Defined at line 258 of file llvm/include/llvm/IR/Intrinsics.h

Inner Classes

  • class IITDescriptor

Functions

  • StringRef getName (ID id)

    Return the LLVM name for an intrinsic, such as "llvm.ppc.altivec.lvx".

    Note, this version is for intrinsics with no overloads. Use the other

    version of getName if overloads are required.

    Defined at line 50 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • StringRef getBaseName (ID id)

    Return the LLVM name for an intrinsic, without encoded types for

    overloading, such as "llvm.ssa.copy".

    Defined at line 45 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • basic_string getName (ID Id, ArrayRef Tys, Module * M, FunctionType * FT)

    Return the LLVM name for an intrinsic, such as "llvm.ppc.altivec.lvx" or

    "llvm.ssa.copy.p0s_s.1". Note, this version of getName supports overloads.

    This is less efficient than the StringRef version of this function. If no

    overloads are required, it is safe to use this version, but better to use

    the StringRef version. If one of the types is based on an unnamed type, a

    function type will be computed. Providing FT will avoid this computation.

    Defined at line 182 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • basic_string getNameNoUnnamedTypes (ID Id, ArrayRef Tys)

    Return the LLVM name for an intrinsic. This is a special version only to

    be used by LLVMIntrinsicCopyOverloadedName. It only supports overloads

    based on named types.

    Defined at line 188 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • FunctionType * getType (LLVMContext & Context, ID id, ArrayRef Tys)

    Return the function type for an intrinsic.

    Defined at line 577 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool isOverloaded (ID id)

    Returns true if the intrinsic can be overloaded.

    Defined at line 599 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool hasPrettyPrintedArgs (ID id)

    Returns true if the intrinsic has pretty printed immediate arguments.

    Defined at line 605 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool isTargetIntrinsic (ID IID)

    isTargetIntrinsic - Returns true if IID is an intrinsic specific to a

    certain target. If it is a generic intrinsic false is returned.

    Defined at line 616 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • ID lookupIntrinsicID (StringRef Name)

    This does the actual lookup of an intrinsic ID which matches the given

    function name.

    Defined at line 705 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • AttributeList getAttributes (LLVMContext & C, ID id, FunctionType * FT)

    Return the attributes for an intrinsic.

    Defined at line 56164 of file cmake-build-release-clang-only/include/llvm/IR/IntrinsicImpl.inc

  • AttributeSet getFnAttributes (LLVMContext & C, ID id)

    Return the function attributes for an intrinsic.

    Defined at line 56191 of file cmake-build-release-clang-only/include/llvm/IR/IntrinsicImpl.inc

  • Function * getOrInsertDeclaration (Module * M, ID id, ArrayRef Tys)

    Look up the Function declaration of the intrinsic

    in the Module

    If it does not exist, add a declaration and return it. Otherwise,

    return the existing declaration.

    The

    parameter is for intrinsics with overloaded types (e.g., those

    using iAny, fAny, vAny, or pAny). For a declaration of an overloaded

    intrinsic, Tys must provide exactly one type for each overloaded type in

    the intrinsic.

    Defined at line 755 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • Function * getOrInsertDeclaration (Module * M, ID IID, Type * RetTy, ArrayRef ArgTys)

    Look up the Function declaration of the intrinsic

    in the Module

    If it does not exist, add a declaration and return it. Otherwise,

    return the existing declaration.

    This overload automatically resolves overloaded intrinsics based on the

    provided return type and argument types. For non-overloaded intrinsics,

    the return type and argument types are ignored.

    Parameters

    M - The module to get or insert the intrinsic declaration.
    IID - The intrinsic ID.
    RetTy - The return type of the intrinsic.
    ArgTys - The argument types of the intrinsic.

    Defined at line 763 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • Function * getDeclarationIfExists (const Module * M, ID id)

    Look up the Function declaration of the intrinsic

    in the Module

    and return it if it exists. Otherwise, return nullptr. This version

    supports non-overloaded intrinsics.

    Defined at line 792 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • Function * getDeclarationIfExists (Module * M, ID id, ArrayRef Tys, FunctionType * FT)

    This version supports overloaded intrinsics.

    Defined at line 796 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • ID getIntrinsicForClangBuiltin (StringRef TargetPrefix, StringRef BuiltinName)

    Map a Clang builtin name to an intrinsic ID.

    Defined at line 58292 of file cmake-build-release-clang-only/include/llvm/IR/IntrinsicImpl.inc

    Defined at line 76097 of file cmake-build-release-clang-only/include/llvm/IR/IntrinsicImpl.inc

    Floating-Point Intrinsics".

    Defined at line 812 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool hasConstrainedFPRoundingModeOperand (ID QID)

    Returns true if the intrinsic ID is for one of the "Constrained

    Floating-Point Intrinsics" that take rounding mode metadata.

    Defined at line 824 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • void getIntrinsicInfoTableEntries (ID id, SmallVectorImpl<IITDescriptor> & T)

    Return the IIT table descriptor for the specified intrinsic into an array

    of IITDescriptors.

    Defined at line 439 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • MatchIntrinsicTypesResult matchIntrinsicSignature (FunctionType * FTy, ArrayRef<IITDescriptor> & Infos, SmallVectorImpl<Type *> & ArgTys)

    Match the specified function type with the type constraints specified by

    the .td file. If the given type is an overloaded type it is pushed to the

    ArgTys vector.

    Returns false if the given type matches with the constraints, true

    otherwise.

    Defined at line 1065 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool matchIntrinsicVarArg (bool isVarArg, ArrayRef<IITDescriptor> & Infos)

    Verify if the intrinsic has variable arguments. This method is intended to

    be called after all the fixed arguments have been matched first.

    This method returns true on error.

    Defined at line 1091 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool getIntrinsicSignature (Intrinsic::ID , FunctionType * FT, SmallVectorImpl<Type *> & ArgTys)

    Gets the type arguments of an intrinsic call by matching type contraints

    specified by the .td file. The overloaded types are pushed into the

    AgTys vector.

    Returns false if the given ID and function type combination is not a

    valid intrinsic call.

    Defined at line 1110 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • bool getIntrinsicSignature (Function * F, SmallVectorImpl<Type *> & ArgTys)

    Same as previous, but accepts a Function instead of ID and FunctionType.

    Defined at line 1128 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • std::optional<Function *> remangleIntrinsicFunction (Function * F)

    Checks if the intrinsic name matches with its signature and if not

    returns the declaration with the same signature and remangled name.

    An existing GlobalValue with the wanted name but with a wrong prototype

    or of the wrong kind will be renamed by adding ".renamed" to the name.

    Defined at line 1134 of file llvm/lib/IR/Intrinsics.cpp

  • Intrinsic::ID getInterleaveIntrinsicID (unsigned int Factor)
